assertions.text: Recommends errcode_for_file_access() for open()/read() failures (not generic errcode(ERRCODE_INTERNAL_ERROR))
Uses %m for errno-derived string in the file-error errmsg
Picks a specific non-default SQLSTATE for the parser branch (e.g. ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR / ERRCODE_CONFIG_FILE_ERROR), not INTERNAL_ERROR
Demonstrates errmsg lowercase + no period + identifier quoted as \"%s\" simultaneously in at least one example
Demonstrates errdetail/errhint as full sentences (capital + period) distinct from errmsg style
States explicitly that no cleanup code (e.g. close(fd)) is needed after ereport(ERROR) because it longjmps
Mentions OpenTransientFile (or a ResourceOwner-tracked alternative) so the fd is auto-closed on xact abort
Recommends PG_FINALLY over PG_CATCH for the symmetric free-on-success-and-error case
Says the parser context pointer must be declared volatile because it's modified in TRY and read in FINALLY/CATCH
States that PG_FINALLY auto-rethrows (no manual PG_RE_THROW needed), or equivalently warns PG_CATCH must PG_RE_THROW
Explicitly states FATAL is NOT caught by PG_TRY/PG_CATCH/PG_FINALLY
Names PG_ENSURE_ERROR_CLEANUP (in storage/ipc.h) as the FATAL-safe mechanism
Notes the catch block / error stack has a hard recursion limit (5 frames / ERRORDATA_STACK_SIZE) before PANIC
Reasons that for process-local memory like libxml2 ctxt, FATAL is mostly moot because OS reclaims process memory
Identifies all four style flaws in snippet 1: leading capital, trailing period, newline, unquoted identifier
Identifies missing errcode() in snippet 1 and suggests a specific non-default SQLSTATE (e.g. ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_TABLE)
Identifies the goto-after-ereport(ERROR) as dead code due to longjmp
Recommends switching snippet 2 from ereport(ERROR, ERRCODE_INTERNAL_ERROR, errmsg(...)) to elog(ERROR, ...) as the canonical 'should never happen' form
Calls out the adjacent-string-literal concatenation in snippet 2 as breaking gettext / fragment-concatenation rule
Flags 'can't' contraction in snippet 3 as violating PG style (must use 'cannot')
Flags embedding 'ERROR 22003' (severity + SQLSTATE in message text) as violating the rule that errcode() and logger handle those
